observations.recommendation:
1.6	We recommended that Management:
Appoint a new Inventory Committee whose members will be temporarily relieved of their regular duties to fully focus on conducting the physical inventory, ensuring its completion within three months as mandated by Section 5.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
Instruct the Inventory Committee to properly plan and execute the inventory taking in strict compliance with the guidelines prescribed in the subject Circular.
Additionally, for those PPEs subject for derecognition, proper documentation should be ensured, and secure proper approval from COA before dropping them from the books to achieve a fair presentation of balances in the financial statements.

2.10	We recommended and the management agreed to:
Release checks immediately to claimants so that all disbursement transactions will be recorded on time by the accountant and that complete and accurate cash balances will be reflected in the books of the agency.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to record in his cashbook only the properly approved and countersigned transactions.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to retrieve the vouchers for the stale check and determine if there is a need to re-issue a replacement check otherwise, effect reversion of the funds to the general fund.

3.12	We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough review of all transferred PPE items below the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold that are still held by the Property Unit and reclassify them under the appropriate Semi-Expendable account.
3.13	Otherwise, all items below the capitalization threshold that were acquired during the year and have already been issued to end-users should be adjusted and recognized as expenses, while those acquired and issued before CY 2024 should be recorded as prior period adjustments. This will ensure proper classification and accurate financial statement balances in compliance with COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024.

4.7	We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant ensure the complete disclosure of details related to the Loans Payable account in the Notes to the Financial Statements including the maturity date, updated interest rate, payment terms and assets pledged against the debt pursuant to paragraph 127 of IPSAS No. 1.

5.9	We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected real and nominal accounts related to the unrecorded receipt of the breeding stocks.

5.16	We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist submit to the COA, a Request for Relief from Accountability so that the Municipal Accountant will have a basis in recording the degeneration of one breeding animal.
5.17	We further recommended that the Accounting and General Services Office maintain the required ledger and property cards necessary in the monitoring of receipt and disposition of breeding animals.

6.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify and confirm the existence and accuracy of the balances of these dormant accounts with the respective source agencies and if deemed necessary, a request for authority to write-off these dormant accounts should be filed following COA Circular No. 2016-005.

7.7	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the actual RPT and SET Receivables  and Deferred RPT and Special Education Tax using the amounts furnished by the Municipal Treasurer.

8.5	We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year as basis in setting up the RPT/ SET receivables and deferred income as instructed by Sec. 20 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System Volume I.
8.6	We further recommended that the suggested adjusting entry be taken up to match the records of the Municipal Treasurer:
GF:
SEF:

9.9	We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate reclassification entries using the aforementioned accounts, in order not to bloat the Accounts Payable Account as of December 31, 2024 and to attain fair presentation of financial statements.

10.6	We recommended that all collections be remitted intact and daily to avoid misuse of government funds.  Likewise, management should call the attention of the above collectors to remind them of their duties and responsibilities; otherwise, they should stop collecting various income payments to ensure safety and proper accountability of government funds.

11.4	We recommended that management stop assigning both collection and disbursing functions to one accountable officer; instead assign the collection function to a Collecting Officer and disbursing function to a Disbursing Officer pursuant to Section 50 of GAAM Volume III.

12.4	We recommended that the LDRRMC maximize the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years by identifying DRRM projects, programs and activities which should be listed in the LDRRMFIP as mandated by Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
13.8	We recommended that the MDRRM Officer strive for the timely and full implementation of LDRRM programs, projects and activities to achieve the goals, objectives and desired benefits of the fund.

14.10	We recommended that Management direct the HRMO to assess whether the tasks assigned to JO workers are genuinely temporary or should be performed by permanent employees. Additionally, they should consider the possibility of filling existing plantilla vacancies or creating additional plantilla positions to promote workforce stability, which could help enhance productivity and ensure service continuity.
14.16	We recommended that Management instruct all the officials and department heads to review and assess the assigned tasks and functions of all Job Order workers and ensure that they are consistently performing duties within their contractual scope of work. Moreover, we recommended that management strictly assign tasks which are duly aligned with government duties, as public funds and personnel should be allocated strictly for government related operations and facilities.

15.8	We recommended that the disbursing officers liquidate cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was granted has been served. We further recommend that no additional cash advances be allowed unless the previous cash advance is first settled.

3. Municipal Accountant records purchases of supplies and materials to its appropriate inventory account and recognize only as expense when the goods are distributed or related services are rendered.  

4. Management maintains the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for supplies in order to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items.

observations.reason: Verified General Ledgers and Financial Statements for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Accountant to sort out the various funds and use them for the furtherance of the improvement of the LGU and implement specific projects as stated therein of each fund received as well as use or return the funds in consonance with COA Circular 2020-04 dated January 20, 2021 and 6.7 of COA Circular No 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, whichever warrants.

We recommend the management to direct its personnel overseeing the implementation of these projects to devise a plan for the immediate execution of these projects and submit liquidation as soon as possible, otherwise, the funds might be transferred earlier to give ample time to the barangays to implement the intended projects.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other officers, work out the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund as programmed to be able to give to the public the benefits they can derive out of the programs/projects/activities aligned for the 20% Development Fund.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to thoroughly review the supporting documents of claims regarding meal and snacks incurred, whether these claims are chargeable against representation, otherwise it will be charged to travelling expenses in the per diem of the travelling official.

We recommend the management to discontinue granting advance payment on 15% mobilization cost on contracts entered into unless such requests are duly supported with an irrevocable standby letter of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand issued by a surety or an insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission pursuant to Annex E of the Revised IIR of RA 9184.

We recommend that management strictly enforce the posting of warranty security by the contractor after the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance pursuant to the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184.
To ensure that the warranty security is posted, the LGU may make it as a requirement before returning the 10% retention money due the contractor one year after the completion of the project after which the warranty period shall commence.

We recommend the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant, GSO, Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008. Prepare appropriate adjusting journal entries to recognize depreciation of the Infrastructure Assets, Books as well as that of the Agricultural and Forestry Equipment accounts.

We recommend the Accounting Office to reconstruct the maintenance/repair record of each vehicle owned and/or operated by the municipal government. Henceforth, require the repair history of motor vehicles before repair work is to be done and also before payment of repair expenses.

We recommend the Human Resource Management Officer to work back and deduct the five (5) days unused mandatory leave credits of all municipal officials and employees in order to arrive at correct accumulated balances. We also recommend that demand of return for the overpaid terminal benefits be made to the payees and furnish copy of the Official Receipt for the refund of overpaid terminal benefits to the Audit Team. Otherwise, the overpaid amount be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that management require the GAD Focal Person to ensure the allocation of at least five percent of the total annual budget of the LGU in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01. Further, we also recommend the management to use applicable tools such as the HGDG for PAPs attributed to the GAD Budget to assure the proper determination of the amounts that may be attributed to the GAD budget.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of recording the partial payment of projects as direct debit to its asset accounts to avoid the subjecting of these assets to depreciation in accordance with Paragraph 59 to 78 of IPSAS 17.

Henceforth, recompute the depreciation account and effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts of the LGU so as to reflect an accurate balance of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: Upon receipt of the AOM, the BAC and Secretariat reviewed all the documents related to the process in the purchased of Drugs and Medicines. In the course of our review we recognized that we were not able to require the submission of the following documents for it was not in the check list provided to us for reference and the COVID-19 Pandemic is the foremost consideration of the BAC in procuring the medicines:                                                     * Certificate of product registration from Food and Drug Administration (FDA)                                                                              * Certificate of good and manufacturing practice from FDA                                                                                                                   *  Batch release from DFA                                                                    *  If the supplier is not the manufacturer that the supplier is an authorized distributor/dealer of the product items                                                                                              With this AOM, it has reminded us to do our review of the eligibility requirements more diligently in our future BAC activities starting March of 2021.  The said requirements were included in the Instruction to Bidders and during the discussion with the prospective bidder/s on the Pre-Bidding Conference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to exert effort to expedite the establishment and completion of the Integrated Solid Waste Management Facility or give priority to its implementation and involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation of the existing environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: This is in connection with the establishment of the Recycling Building at the proposed Jagna Integrated Eco Center Facility in Tubod Mar, Jagna, Bohol. The project construction started last February 15, 2021 with a duration of 180 calendar days. After the issuance of the Notice to Proceed (NTP) to CEVIC Construction and Development Corporation, the implementation got so smooth until in the month of March when a ground breaking of another project was conducted. An heir of the procured lot by the LGU for the access road wanted to close the area of their land because she claimed that she was not aware of the negotiation for the procurement of the lot and that she would also be filling a complaint to the barangay. The construction was suspended on April until September 2021. On November, the RTC Branch 7 in Loay dismissed the case against the municipality which paved way to the issuance of a new ECC for Jagna Integrated Eco Center Facility. Construction resumed but was again halted on December 16, 2021 due to the Super Typhoon Odette that ravaged the whole province of Bohol. Nonetheless, it was recorded that the project was presently at its 65% accomplishment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in preparation, approving of trip tickets as well as authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip.  Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Fuel Consumption Report for each vehicle and submit the same to the auditor.

Furthermore, everytime the vehicle/heavy equipment shall be refueled at the refilling station, a checker/inspector should accompany and check whether the correct quantity of fuel being issued is the same as that pumped into the vehicle of heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive designate Mr. Raymond Emmanuel F. Cuadra, Municipal Treasurer as GSO in charge of all LGU assets including motor vehicle-gasoline and repairs and maintenance.  There were proper documents attached to support the trips.
observations.reason:
Validation disclosed attached trip tickets deficient of data i.e. balance of fuel in tankbefore the trip, balance of fuel in tank after the trip, number of kilometers per distance travelled.

There was no report of Monthly Report of Official Travel and Monthly Consumption.

Also, require the driver/personnel concerned to subject the damaged parts of the vehicle to pre-repair inspection before requisition for replacement is made and to the replaced parts to post repair inspection by the designated LGU Inspector to ascertain that the vehicle is in operational condition.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive  designates Mr. Raymond Emmanuel F. Cuadra as GSO and incharged of all repairs and maintenance to review and monitor repairs whether such requests are valid and needed by the motor vehicle.
observations.reason: Validation of the claims of repairs and maintenance disclosed incomplete supporting documents i.e. pre-repair inspection reports, post-repair inspection reports and waste material reports.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that –

      1) The Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee  to  give
         focus on the task of preparing the  Inventory  Report  and  comply
         with the provision of Section 124 of the NGAS.

      2) The Municipal Accountant immediately  effect  adjustments  of  the
         discrepancies, if any, between the actual and recorded balances of
         the affected Property, Plant and Equipment accounts based  on  the
         results of the actual physical count.


      3) Prepare and maintain all pertinent subsidiary record for Property,
         Plant and Equipment account.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of
      Section 4(5) of PD 1445, and to resolve first  whatever  issues  that
      cause the absence of  the  proper  signatories  on  the  disbursement
      vouchers.  The Municipal Treasurer has to see to it that  all  claims
      bear the approval/complete signatories before payment is effected.

We recommend that management require all officials and employees
      concerned and responsible in preparation, approving of trip tickets as
      well as authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket  is
      duly and properly accomplished and  approved  before  undertaking  the
      trip.  Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official
      Travel and the Fuel Consumption Report for each vehicle and submit the
      same to the auditor.

           Furthermore, everytime  the  vehicle/heavy  equipment  shall  be
      refueled  at  the  refilling  station,  a   checker/inspector   should
      accompany and check whether the correct quantity of fuel being  issued
      is the same as that pumped into the vehicle or heavy equipment.

We recommend  that  Management  instruct  the  Bids  and  Awards
      Committee to furnish the Audit  Team  with  the  copies  of  perfected
      contracts for infrastructure projects and purchase  orders  for  goods
      procured,  together  with  the  integral  parts/supporting   documents
      thereof, within five (5) days from perfection thereof  to  enable  the
      Audit Team to conduct the auditorial/legal review of  these  documents
      at the most appropriate time and manner pursuant to COA  Circular  No.
      2009-002.  Further, instruct the Inspection  Officer  to  Inspectorate
      Team to furnish copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports,  together
      with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within  twenty-four  (24)
      hours from acceptance of the items delivered to enable the Audit  Team
      to conduct timely validation/inspection thereof, pursuant  to  Section
      6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 .

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant  strictly  adhere  to
      the provision of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 and
      see to it that all claims are supported with complete documentation to
      ensure that government funds are expended for valid, proper and  legal
      purposes.


           Further, instruct the concerned officials and  department  heads
      to affix their signature only after thorough review of the  supporting
      documents have been made before payment is effected.

We recommend that management explain in writing  the  indication
      of technical specification and description that refer to a brand  name
      in  the  procurement  of  the  vehicle  and  to  strictly  follow  the
      provisions of Section 18 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend the  management  to  comply  with  the  documentary
      requirements for repairs, and maintain records for each motor vehicle.
       Also, require the driver/personnel concerned to subject  the  damaged
      parts of the vehicle to pre-repair inspection before  requisition  for
      replacement  is  made  and  to  the  replaced  parts  to  post  repair
      inspection by the designated  LGU  Inspector  to  ascertain  that  the
      vehicle is in operational condition.

We recommend that management closely  monitor  and  ensure  that
      LDRRM funds are utilized in accordance with  the  plan  and  give  due
      regard  on  the  maximum  utilization  of  the  70%  for  pre-disaster
      preparedness   programs   so   that   the    intended    purpose    is
      attained/achieved.

We recommend that the Management:

     1. Require the  Municipal  Accountant  to  debit  Cash  in  Bank-Local
        Currency, Current/Savings Accounts and credit Accounts Payable  for
        P228,329.24 to restore the liability to creditors upon presentation
        of the stale checks.


     2. Require the Municipal Treasurer to  send  written  notices  to  the
        payees of the existence of the checks  and  inform  the  payees  at
        least one month before a check becomes stale.

We recommend that the local chief executive require the  persons
      liable   to   immediately   settle   the   disallowances,   otherwise
      administrative and/or criminal action can be filed  against  them  in
      line with Section 7.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated  September
      15, 2009 Re: Prescribing the use of the Rules and Regulations on  the
      Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of recording the payment from fund transfers as direct debit to the Trust Liability Account and make the necessary adjustments in the books of the agency using the prescribed pro forma journal entries in both Trust Fund and General Fund Books.  Further, depreciation expense has to be set up to reflect the accurate value of the property.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made.

Recommendation

It is recommended that –

1) The Local Chief Executive directs the Inventory Committee to  give  focus
   on the task of  preparing  the  Inventory  Report  and  comply  with  the
   provision of Section 124 of the NGAS.

2) The  Municipal  Accountant  to  immediately  effect  adjustments  of  the
   discrepancies, if any, between the actual and recorded  balances  of  the
   affected Property, Plant and Equipment accounts based on the  results  of
   the actual physical count.


3) Prepare and maintain all pertinent subsidiary record for Property,  Plant
   and Equipment account.

Recommendation

      We recommend that management stop the practice of procuring goods on a
staggered basis and through reimbursements, and adhere to the provisions  of
Section 10 of RA 9184.  Limit the use of  alternative  mode  of  procurement
only when circumstances warrant and fall within the  provisions  of  Section
49-53 of RA 9184.

Recommendation:

      The Municipal Officials have to  limit  the  utilization  of  the  20%
Development  Fund  to  viable  and  sustainable   programs,   projects   and
activities that directly generate jobs and livelihood opportunities  to  its
constituents in accordance with Department of Interior and Local  Government
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

Recommendation:

     We recommend that management  closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM
planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due  regard  on
the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre  disaster  preparedness  programs
so that  intended purpose  is attained/achieved.

Recommendation

      We recommend management to stop the practice of holding  out  of  town
seminars and conferences on activities that can be held within the locality
and exercise prudence  in  the  utilization  of  the  municipality’s  scarce
resources.

Recommendation

      We recommend to the LGU official concerned to attach lists of  RPT/SET
receivables  to  support   the   aforementioned   account   balances,   send
confirmation letters to national government agencies  which  are  listed  as
well as those names included in “other  receivable”  account  identified  as
debtors and/or demand payment thereof.  Henceforth,  request  write  off  of
those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission  on  Audit
pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Report of Physical Count of Inventory, Property, Plant and Equipment be submitted to this Office for our determination of the correctness of the reported balances of these accounts as well as in adherence to the prescribed law under Section 124 of the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units (NGAS).
observations.managementAction: An inventory was conducted and partial report was submitted.
observations.reason: Some of the inventories listed have to be reconciled with Accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of the priority development projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund during the year the funds are actually appropriated in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and the desired outcomes from the said programs or projects.
observations.managementAction: PPAs were implemented and some are ongoing.
observations.reason: Some PPAs were not implemented due to time constraints.

observations.recommendation: In view of the SC’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF and all informal practices of similar impact and effect, we recommend that  all appropriation shall incorporated in the general AIP and PPMP of the Municipal Government and implemented by concerned department.
observations.managementAction: Generic allocations to other offices are ceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to instruct the Bids and Award Committee(BAC) to see to it that the documentary requirements for drugs and medicines be included in the eligbility requirements of supplier/s. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant must see to it that disbursement vouchers are supported with the above mentioned documents as required under Section .1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Lacking documents which were the subject of the audit observation are now attached to the vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop charging expenses not align with the identified purpose of the Special Education Fund and require the Local School Board to adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of Republic Act 7160 and Section 83, Chapter 5 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I in identifying the programs and activities to be funded out of the fund. Furthermore, advise the Local School Board to make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan for the possible appropriation in the General Fund of the expenditures deemed necessary in the operation of the school but are not included among the purposes provided for under RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The management stopped the charges of expenditures which are not among those identified by Section 272 of RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the cashbook and accounting records every quarter in order to arrive to an accurate and reliable account balances at any given period and likewise present a fair and accurate financial statements.  For the unreconciled balances of prior years  instruct the Municipal Accountant and her staff to work back on these accounts and  effect the necessary adjustments on the discrepancies .
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconciled balances of the ledgers against the cashbooks.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to instruct the Municipal Engineer to post signage /billboard at the project site and should see to it that it is in accordance with the standard form as required under COA Circular 2013-004. Moreover,  the Local Chief Executive  should see to it that the concerned Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader are informed of the posting within ten days after award of the project as stipulated in Section 3.1 of the same circular.
observations.managementAction: Billboards are erected in place where the projects are located however, some does not conformed to the required standard form as required under COA Circular 2013-004.
observations.reason: The management overlooked to follow the standard form required by COA Circular 2013-004.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to furnish the Audit Team with the copies of perfected contracts for infrastructure projects and purchase orders for goods procured, together with the integral parts/supporting documents thereof, within five (5) days from perfection thereof to enable the Audit Team to conduct the auditorial/legal review of these documents at the most appropriate time and manner pursuant to COA Circular No. 89-299.  Further, instruct the Inspection Officer or Inspectorate Team to furnish copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely validation/inspection thereof, pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: Only purchase orders were submitted to COA but not the approved contracts for infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: The BAC was not aware that they have to send copies of approved contracts to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge expenses to its appropriate expense account within the approved budget to have a fair presentation of the financial statements.
